drill: OP-4.3 — executed restore drill for raw SourceMessage data (D-055)

metadata.notes: Reference/local docker-compose shape (docker-compose.yml, pgvector/pgvector:pg16). Synthetic source_messages rows only — community_id 'op43-drill', no PII, no real payloads. Restored into the dedicated memory_rag_pg_restore_data scratch volume via scripts/pg_restore/restore.sh. Inspection harness, not a make check gate.

ingestion.comment: Three synthetic batches ingested AFTER the base backup so recovery depends on archived-WAL replay. Batches 1 and 2 forced a WAL switch (pg_switch_wal); batch 3 did not, to observe the un-archived window.

source_message_check.expected: all 3 batches (15 rows) — every batch's WAL was archived by restore time

restore_pitr.rpo_seconds_or_window: 0 — the recovery point (between batch-1 and batch-2) was reached exactly; all data committed at or before the target was recovered, all data after it correctly excluded

source_message_check.expected: batch-1 only (5 rows); batch-2 and batch-3 excluded by recovery_target_time

rpo_observation.comment: RPO is the data-loss window between the last recoverable point and a failure. A forced pg_switch_wal archives a segment immediately (RPO -> 0). Without a switch, archive_timeout=300s force-archives a partially-filled segment, bounding the un-archived window.
rpo_observation.unforced_window_detail: batch-3 committed 2026-05-19T06:40:23Z with no forced switch; its WAL segment 000000010000000000000006 was force-archived at 2026-05-19T06:45:13Z by archive_timeout=300 — ~290s, within the 300s bound.

summary.rpo_basis: worst-case un-archived window for an un-switched raw write, bounded by archive_timeout=300s; forced-switch and post-archive_timeout data observed at 0 loss
summary.rto_basis: longest observed restore wall-clock (full restore); PITR observed at 3s
summary.phase8_raw_durability_dod: met — raw SourceMessage rows recovered from the prior backup window (full restore) and to a tighter-than-nightly recovery point (PITR), both within the D-053 RPO/RTO targets

log_reference: /archive/restore_logs/ in the memory_rag_pg_archive volume — restore.sh writes restore-<ts>.log and last_restore.json per run (transient: the drill volumes were dropped at teardown).
